---
title: "Técnicas de Previsión"
author: "José, Oier y Marc"
date: "2025-12-28"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Estudio y Análisis Previo de la Serie SIN COVID

##Lectura de los Datos
```{r}
# 1. Leer los datos
datos = read.csv("Turistas_Limpio.csv")

# 2. Identificar la fila donde empieza 2022
datos_post = datos[74:nrow(datos), ] 

# 3. Crear la serie temporal con los datos filtrados
ts_limpia = ts(
  datos_post$Total,
  start = c(2022, 1),
  frequency = 12
)
```

##Visualización de la Serie Temporal
```{r}
plot(ts_limpia,
     main = "Serie Post-Covid: Evolución de Turistas",
     ylab = "Total",
     xlab = "Año",
     col = "blue",
     lwd = 2)
```

##Descomposición de la Serie Temporal
### Visualización individual de cada componente
```{r fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
descomp_limpia = stl(ts_limpia, s.window = "periodic")

tendencia      = descomp_limpia$time.series[, "trend"]
estacionalidad = descomp_limpia$time.series[, "seasonal"]
ruido          = descomp_limpia$time.series[, "remainder"]

par(mfrow = c(3, 1), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 2))

plot(tendencia, type="l", col="blue", lwd=2,
     main="Tendencia estimada (sin COVID)",
     ylab="Tendencia", xlab="Tiempo")

plot(estacionalidad, type="l", col="darkgreen", lwd=2,
     main="Componente estacional (sin COVID)",
     ylab="Estacionalidad", xlab="Tiempo")

plot(ruido, type="h", col="gray40",
     main="Componente irregular (sin COVID)",
     ylab="Residuo", xlab="Tiempo")
abline(h=0, col="red", lty=2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```


##Visualización Varianza por Período
```{r,warning=FALSE}
library(zoo)

var_movil_limpia = rollapply(ts_limpia, width=12, FUN=var, align="right", fill=NA)

plot(var_movil_limpia,
     type="l",
     col="red",
     lwd=2,
     main="Varianza móvil 12 meses (sin COVID)",
     ylab="Varianza",
     xlab="Tiempo")
```


## Medias Móviles

```{r}
library(forecast)

# Calculamos medias móviles (Escala Original)
ma3_fitted  = ma(ts_limpia, order = 3)
ma6_fitted  = ma(ts_limpia, order = 6)
ma12_fitted = ma(ts_limpia, order = 12)
```

```{r}
# Graficar con leyenda y márgenes homogéneos
rango_ma = range(as.numeric(ts_limpia), na.rm = TRUE)
plot(ts_limpia, 
     main = "Comparativa de Medias Móviles (Periodo Post-COVID)", 
     ylab = "Turistas", 
     xlab = "Tiempo", 
     col = "grey70", 
     lwd = 1,
     ylim = c(rango_ma[1], rango_ma[2] * 1.3))

lines(ma3_fitted, col = "blue", lwd = 2) 
lines(ma6_fitted, col = "red", lwd = 2) 
lines(ma12_fitted, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)

legend("topleft", 
       legend = c("Serie Original", "MA(3)", "MA(6)", "MA(12)"), 
       col = c("grey70", "blue", "red", "darkgreen"), 
       lwd = 2, 
       bty = "n", 
       cex = 0.8)
```


##Modelo SES, Holt y Holt-Winter
```{r}
# SES
ses_model <- HoltWinters(ts_limpia, beta = FALSE, gamma = FALSE)
ses_fitted <- ses_model$fitted[,1]

# Holt
holt_model <- HoltWinters(ts_limpia, gamma = FALSE)
holt_fitted <- holt_model$fitted[,1]

# Holt-Winter Aditivo (Escala Original)
hw_add <- HoltWinters(ts_limpia, seasonal = "additive")
hw_add_fitted <- hw_add$fitted[,1]
```


```{r}
# Gráfico SES con margen para leyenda
rango_ses = range(c(as.numeric(ts_limpia), as.numeric(ses_fitted)), na.rm = TRUE)
plot(ts_limpia,
     main = "Suavizado Exponencial Simple (SES)",
     ylab = "Total", xlab = "Tiempo",
     col = "black", lwd = 2,
     ylim = c(rango_ses[1], rango_ses[2] * 1.3))

lines(ses_fitted, col = "blue", lwd = 2)

legend("topleft",
       legend = c("Serie original Post Covid", "SES"),
       col = c("black", "blue"),
       lwd = 2,
       bty = "n")
```


```{r}
# Gráfico Holt con margen para leyenda
rango_holt = range(c(as.numeric(ts_limpia), as.numeric(holt_fitted)), na.rm = TRUE)
plot(ts_limpia, 
     main = "Modelo de Holt (Tendencia): Serie Post COVID", 
     ylab = "Total", xlab = "Tiempo", 
     col = "black", lwd = 2,
     ylim = c(rango_holt[1], rango_holt[2] * 1.3))

lines(holt_fitted, col = "red", lwd = 2)

legend("topleft", 
       legend = c("Serie original Post Covid", "Holt"),
       col = c("black", "red"), 
       lwd = 2, 
       bty = "n")
```


```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
# Gráfico Holt-Winter con margen para leyenda
rango_hw = range(c(as.numeric(ts_limpia), as.numeric(hw_add_fitted)), na.rm = TRUE)
plot(ts_limpia, 
     main = "Holt-Winters Aditivo: Serie Post COVID", 
     ylab = "Total", xlab = "Tiempo", 
     col = "black", lwd = 2,
     ylim = c(rango_hw[1], rango_hw[2] * 1.3))

lines(hw_add_fitted, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)

legend("topleft", 
       legend = c("Serie original Post Covid", "Holt-Winters Aditivo"),
       col = c("black", "darkgreen"), 
       lwd = 2, 
       bty = "n")
```

```{r}
library(forecast)

hw_pred_sombra = forecast(hw_add, h = 12)

pred_filt = predict(hw_add, n.ahead = 12, prediction.interval = TRUE, level = 0.95)

plot(hw_pred_sombra, 
     main = "Pronóstico Holt-Winters: Bandas de Confianza",
     ylab = "Total Turistas", xlab = "Tiempo",
     fcol = "red", 
     shadecols = c("#E0E0E0", "#BDBDBD"), 
     col = "black", lwd = 1.5)

# Ver métricas de error
accuracy(hw_pred_sombra)
```

```{r}
# Gráfico de filtering
plot(hw_add, 
     predicted.values = pred_filt, 
     main = "Holt-Winters filtering: Serie Post COVID",
     ylab = "Observed / Fitted", xlab = "Time",
     col = "black", 
     col.predicted = "blue", 
     lwd = 1)

abline(v = tail(time(ts_limpia), 1), lty = 3, col = "gray40")
```

```{r}
# Métricas de error (escala original)
# 1. Definición de las funciones de error
MAE  = function(y, yhat) mean(abs(y - yhat))
RMSE = function(y, yhat) sqrt(mean((y - yhat)^2))
MAPE = function(y, yhat) mean(abs((y - yhat) / y)) * 100

y_real = window(ts_limpia, start = start(hw_add_fitted))

resultados = data.frame(
  Modelo = c("SES", "Holt", "Holt-Winters"),
  MAE = c(
    MAE(y_real, ses_fitted),
    MAE(y_real, holt_fitted),
    MAE(y_real, hw_add_fitted)
  ),
  RMSE = c(
    RMSE(y_real, ses_fitted),
    RMSE(y_real, holt_fitted),
    RMSE(y_real, hw_add_fitted)
  ),
  MAPE = c(
    MAPE(y_real, ses_fitted),
    MAPE(y_real, holt_fitted),
    MAPE(y_real, hw_add_fitted)
  )
)

library(knitr)
kable(resultados, 
      caption = "Métricas de Evaluación: Periodo Post-COVID (Serie Aditiva)",
      digits = 2)
```
